name: Descent across targetLow boundary
description: BG descends from 13 down through the targetLow boundary (6.0) over 3 hours. The critical section is 17:10-18:10 where BG crosses from 8.9 → 8.0 → 7.4 → 7.9 → 6.9 → 8.2 → 10.2. This tests that the engine does NOT double-nudge when BG dips to 7.4 (in-target) and then further to 6.9 (below-target) within the same continuous dip — the category changes from in-target-falling to below, but it's the same dip and the absorption window from the first nudge should suppress the second. Extracted from real 2026-04-09 data where this was a live bug (double-nudge caused user to eat 15g fast sugar total, overshooting).
